description_long: This is a mid-rise apartment building with a rectangular footprint and a flat roof, typical of early-to-mid 20th century European urban architecture. The facade is finished in a muted, weathered gray, with six stories of symmetrically arranged windows, each featuring simple white frames and some with partially open curtains. The ground floor is distinguished by a stone or concrete base with small windows and a single main entrance door, while the upper floors have larger, regularly spaced windows. The building lacks balconies or decorative elements, emphasizing a utilitarian and functional design. The exterior walls appear to be constructed from concrete or stucco over masonry, with subtle signs of aging and urban wear. The overall appearance suggests a residential structure designed for multiple families, blending into a dense city block.
description: A six-story, gray, rectangular apartment building with symmetrical windows and a flat roof, featuring a simple, utilitarian facade and a single main entrance.
description_view_0: This view shows the main facade with the entrance door at the bottom center, a stone base, and six rows of symmetrically aligned windows.
description_view_1: This side is nearly identical to the front but lacks a visible entrance, maintaining the same window arrangement and stone base.
description_view_2: This view displays the rear or another side of the building, with the same window pattern and no visible entrance or distinguishing features.
description_view_3: This side mirrors the previous views, with consistent window placement and no additional architectural details.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a highly symmetrical, grid-like pattern with even vertical and horizontal spacing across all visible facades.
